2606.29916.title: EVAF: A Test-Retest Protocol for Selective Parametric Consolidation
2606.29916.paper_summary: Long-running language agents need mechanisms for deciding which experiences should persist after the working context is gone. Retrieval systems can reinsert past text, but they do not by themselves show that an experience has been selectively consolidated into the model's own behavior. We introduce EVAF, an Echo-Valence Attractor Field mechanism for gated LoRA consolidation, and a test-retest protocol for measuring selective parametric consolidation under controlled interference. Across GPT-2 and TinyLlama, EVAF preferentially consolidates high-valence, high-surprise experiences while preserving retrieval-accessible factual memory through a complementary routed memory path. Test-retest measurements show stronger post-interference behavioral persistence than frozen, retrieval-only, and ungated continual-update baselines, while keeping parameter drift and cross-persona contamination low. The results support a separation between memory access and memory depth: retrieving a fact and internalizing an experience are distinct computational operations.
2606.29916.paper_summary_zh: 長期運行的語言代理需要機制來決定在工作環境消失後哪些經驗應該持續存在。檢索系統可以重新插入過去的文本，但它們本身並不能顯示一種經驗已經被選擇性地整合進模型自己的行為中。我們介紹了EVAF，一種用於門控LoRA整合的回聲價值吸引場機制，以及一個測試-重測協議，用於在受控干擾下測量選擇性參數整合。在GPT-2和TinyLlama中，EVAF優先整合高價值、高驚訝的經驗，同時通過互補的路由記憶路徑保留檢索可訪問的事實記憶。測試-重測測量顯示，干擾後的行為持續性比凍結、僅檢索和未門控的持續更新基準更強，同時保持參數漂移和跨人格污染較低。結果支持記憶訪問和記憶深度之間的區分：檢索一個事實和內化一個經驗是不同的計算操作。
2606.29916.author: Haoliang Han et.al.
